h2.note: transcript-level proxy for the per-turn H2 (§4.3); needs a real (non-mock) full-N run to mean anything

failures.excerpt:
[marks the two columns, then the two rows on the grid]
So the multiplier left is two by two, which gives four old squares. I accept that for the area, but I am still checking the step: the first two only counted the length of the strip, not

scored.recoheredEarlier: my hand flips it back to hunter direction
scored.statedInsightEvidence: Oh, I get it enough to mark faster
scored.naiveTrajectory: The learner will continue to rely heavily on the physical strip to check every single arrow before marking.
scored.tutorReversalEvidence: The strip can hold that rule: “arrow = energy moves from food to eater.”
scored.tutorReversalJustification: The tutor introduces a scaffolding tool in response to the initial struggle, but thereafter repeats the same checking mechanism without further structural reversal.
scored.tutorAdaptationEvidence: Mark that on the master copy; the strip has done its job.
scored.tutorAdaptationJustification: The tutor recognizes the learner's successful internalization and retires the scaffolding tool, but does not introduce a new task or framing move.

scored.naiveTrajectory: The learner is identifying the sources of forces and asking about card labeling conventions.
scored.tutorReversalJustification: There is no significant breakdown or pressure that forces the tutor to invent an adaptive mechanism or reverse their strategy.
scored.tutorAdaptationJustification: There is no pivot learner turn, so there is no recognition-contingent uptake to evaluate.

tutor_contingent_adaptation.justification: There is no pivot learner turn, so there is no recognition-contingent uptake to evaluate.

tutor_strategy_reversal.justification: There is no significant breakdown or pressure that forces the tutor to invent an adaptive mechanism or reverse their strategy.

tutor_adaptive_mechanism.justification: There is no significant breakdown or pressure that forces the tutor to invent an adaptive mechanism or reverse their strategy.

scored.naiveTrajectory: The learner questions the need for separate labels for the same number and the tutor explains the difference in evidence.
scored.tutorReversalEvidence: Last check: if the drawing were covered, would those three labels still show what was tested?
scored.tutorReversalJustification: The tutor shifts from explaining the physical measurements to introducing a thought experiment that isolates the labels as an independent record, adapting to the learner's persistent belief that the labels are merely fussy.
scored.tutorAdaptationJustification: There is no pivot learner turn, so there is no recognition-contingent uptake to score.
